# NeuGo layer constructor reference

Every exported `nn` package constructor (a function returning a single `*Xxx` layer/module pointer), auto-extracted from source so this list can't silently drift from the code. For prose, design rationale, and worked examples, see [`GUIDE.md`](GUIDE.md).

## `AdaptiveAvgPool2D`

```go
AdaptiveAvgPool2D(outH, outW int) *AdaptiveAvgPool2DLayer
```

## `AvgPool2D`

```go
AvgPool2D(poolSize, stride int) *AvgPool2DLayer
```

## `BatchNorm`

```go
BatchNorm(channels int) *BatchNormLayer
```

## `Conv1D`

```go
Conv1D(rng *rand.Rand, inChannels, outChannels, kernelSize int, init Initializer) *Conv1DLayer
```

Conv1D is stride-1, zero-padding ("valid").

## `Conv1DSame`

```go
Conv1DSame(rng *rand.Rand, inChannels, outChannels, kernelSize int, init Initializer) *Conv1DLayer
```

Conv1DSame is stride-1 with padding (kernelSize-1)/2 ("same"); kernelSize
must be odd.

## `Conv1DStrided`

```go
Conv1DStrided(rng *rand.Rand, inChannels, outChannels, kernelSize, stride, padding int, init Initializer) *Conv1DLayer
```

Conv1DStrided exposes explicit stride and padding control.

## `Conv2D`

```go
Conv2D(rng *rand.Rand, inChannels, outChannels, kernelSize int, init Initializer) *Conv2DLayer
```

Conv2D is stride-1, zero-padding ("valid").

## `Conv2DSame`

```go
Conv2DSame(rng *rand.Rand, inChannels, outChannels, kernelSize int, init Initializer) *Conv2DLayer
```

Conv2DSame is stride-1 with padding (kernelSize-1)/2 ("same"); kernelSize
must be odd.

## `Conv2DStrided`

```go
Conv2DStrided(rng *rand.Rand, inChannels, outChannels, kernelSize, stride, padding int, init Initializer) *Conv2DLayer
```

Conv2DStrided exposes explicit stride and padding control â€” e.g. a
stride-2 1x1 or 3x3 conv for ResNet-style downsampling shortcuts, where
neither Conv2D's fixed stride-1/no-padding nor Conv2DSame's fixed
stride-1/same-padding apply.

## `ConvTranspose2D`

```go
ConvTranspose2D(rng *rand.Rand, inChannels, outChannels, kernelSize, stride, padding int, init Initializer) *ConvTranspose2DLayer
```

ConvTranspose2D does not support output_padding (the PyTorch parameter
that resolves ambiguous output sizes for stride > 1); its output size
is exactly (in-1)*stride - 2*padding + kernelSize.

## `CrossAttention`

```go
CrossAttention(rng *rand.Rand, dModel, numHeads int, init Initializer) *CrossAttentionLayer
```

## `Dropout`

```go
Dropout(rate float32) *DropoutLayer
```

## `ELU`

```go
ELU(alpha float32) *ActivationModule
```

ELU: f(x) = x for x>0, alpha*(exp(x)-1) otherwise.

## `Embedding`

```go
Embedding(rng *rand.Rand, vocabSize, embedDim int, init Initializer) *EmbeddingLayer
```

Embedding maps indices â€” [batch, seqLen] of float32 values holding
non-negative integers â€” to dense vectors: output [batch, seqLen,
embedDim]. Indices are not differentiable, so Backward always returns
an all-zero input gradient; instead it scatter-accumulates into W.Grad,
summing gradOut's row into every index it saw so a repeated index
correctly picks up gradient contributions from every occurrence. init
defaults to N(0, 1) when nil, matching common embedding-table init.

## `Flatten`

```go
Flatten() *FlattenLayer
```

## `Frozen`

```go
Frozen(inner Module) *FrozenModule
```

Frozen wraps inner so its Params are excluded from training.

## `GELU`

```go
GELU() *ActivationModule
```

GELU uses the exact formula 0.5*x*(1+erf(x/sqrt(2))), not the SiLU
approximation the old Network/nnx.go mislabeled as GELU.

## `GRU`

```go
GRU(rng *rand.Rand, features, hidden int, init Initializer) *GRULayer
```

## `GlobalAvgPool2D`

```go
GlobalAvgPool2D() *AdaptiveAvgPool2DLayer
```

GlobalAvgPool2D averages every spatial position down to a single value
per channel â€” AdaptiveAvgPool2D(1, 1).

## `GlobalMaxPool2D`

```go
GlobalMaxPool2D() *GlobalMaxPool2DLayer
```

## `GroupNorm`

```go
GroupNorm(groups, channels int) *GroupNormLayer
```

## `Hardswish`

```go
Hardswish() *ActivationModule
```

Hardswish: f(x) = x*relu6(x+3)/6 â€” a piecewise-linear approximation of
SiLU used by MobileNetV3.

## `InstanceNorm`

```go
InstanceNorm(channels int) *InstanceNormLayer
```

## `LSTM`

```go
LSTM(rng *rand.Rand, features, hidden int, init Initializer) *LSTMLayer
```

## `LastTimestep`

```go
LastTimestep() *LastTimestepLayer
```

## `LayerNorm`

```go
LayerNorm(channels int) *LayerNormLayer
```

## `LeakyReLU`

```go
LeakyReLU(alpha float32) *ActivationModule
```

## `Linear`

```go
Linear(rng *rand.Rand, inFeatures, outFeatures int, init Initializer) *LinearLayer
```

Linear creates a dense layer. inFeatures == 0 defers weight allocation
until OutputShape is called with the real preceding shape (see design
decision #2 in the plan header).

## `MaxPool2D`

```go
MaxPool2D(poolSize, stride int) *MaxPool2DLayer
```

## `Mish`

```go
Mish() *ActivationModule
```

Mish: f(x) = x*tanh(softplus(x)).

## `MultiHeadAttention`

```go
MultiHeadAttention(rng *rand.Rand, dModel, numHeads int, causal bool, init Initializer) *MultiHeadAttentionLayer
```

## `NewParam`

```go
NewParam(value *Tensor) *Param
```

## `NewTensor`

```go
NewTensor(shape []int) *Tensor
```

## `PReLU`

```go
PReLU(channels int) *PReLULayer
```

PReLU creates a PReLU layer over channels, with Alpha initialized to
0.25 per channel (the default from the original PReLU paper).

## `PositionalEmbedding`

```go
PositionalEmbedding(rng *rand.Rand, maxLen, dModel int, init Initializer) *PositionalEmbeddingLayer
```

PositionalEmbedding builds position indices 0..seqLen-1 internally on
every Forward call â€” callers never construct them. Forward errors if
seqLen exceeds maxLen rather than truncating or indexing out of bounds.

## `RMSNorm`

```go
RMSNorm(channels int) *RMSNormLayer
```

## `RNN`

```go
RNN(rng *rand.Rand, features, hidden int, init Initializer) *RNNLayer
```

## `ReLU`

```go
ReLU() *ActivationModule
```

## `Residual`

```go
Residual(shortcut Module, inner ...Module) *ResidualBlock
```

Residual builds a block that computes inner(x) + shortcut(x) (or
inner(x) + x if shortcut is nil). Use a nil shortcut when inner preserves
x's shape (the common case); pass a projection module â€” e.g.
Conv2DStrided with a 1x1 kernel â€” when inner changes channels and/or
spatial size, so the shortcut's output shape matches inner's.

## `RotaryMultiHeadAttention`

```go
RotaryMultiHeadAttention(rng *rand.Rand, dModel, numHeads int, causal bool, init Initializer) *RotaryMultiHeadAttentionLayer
```

## `SELU`

```go
SELU() *ActivationModule
```

SELU is scaled ELU with the fixed constants above â€” no configurable
alpha, unlike ELU.

## `SiLU`

```go
SiLU() *ActivationModule
```

SiLU (aka Swish): f(x) = x*sigmoid(x).

## `Sigmoid`

```go
Sigmoid() *ActivationModule
```

## `Softmax`

```go
Softmax() *SoftmaxModule
```

## `Softplus`

```go
Softplus() *ActivationModule
```

Softplus: f(x) = log(1+exp(x)); deriv = sigmoid(x).

## `Tanh`

```go
Tanh() *ActivationModule
```

## `newActivation`

```go
newActivation(name string, alpha float32, fn activationFn) *ActivationModule
```

## `newConv1D`

```go
newConv1D(rng *rand.Rand, inChannels, outChannels, kernelSize, padding, stride int, init Initializer) *Conv1DLayer
```

## `newConv2D`

```go
newConv2D(rng *rand.Rand, inChannels, outChannels, kernelSize, padding, stride int, init Initializer) *Conv2DLayer
```
